skill.name: baseline-ui
skill.description: Enforces practical UI quality rules for AI-assisted frontend work.
skill.summary: Applies a focused baseline for accessible, stable, and polished UI implementation.
skill.author: ibelick

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but these are false positives from Markdown inline code and ordinary UI terms. The skill contains documentation-only guidance and no executable scripts, shell commands, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection. One external documentation link is present, so the final risk is low and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Inline Markdown Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The reported command execution locations are Markdown inline code terms such as command examples, package names, CSS utilities, and component names. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is plain Markdown guidance, and the flagged backtick content is rendered documentation. I found no evidence of shell execution, script files, or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Ordinary UI Words Misclassified as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts map to normal prose or headings, including the skill description, AlertDialog guidance, animation guidance, and the Design heading. No cryptographic API, hashing algorithm, or host reconnaissance behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain UI documentation text, not code. I found no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1 use, system probing, environment inspection, or filesystem enumeration.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The skill links to Base UI component documentation. This is not an active network request or exfiltration path, but marketplace reviewers should note that users may leave the local environment when following the link.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is directly visible and points to public documentation. It does not execute automatically or transmit data.

content.user_title: Improve UI Quality with Baseline Rules
content.value_statement: AI-generated interfaces often miss accessibility, layout, animation, and polish details. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear checklist for building or reviewing frontend UI.
actual_capabilities: Applies baseline rules for Tailwind CSS, component primitives, interaction states, animation, typography, layout, performance, and visual design.
Reviews a UI file and reports violations with the exact snippet, a short reason, and a concrete fix.
Encourages accessible primitives for keyboard and focus behavior.
Promotes restrained animation that uses compositor properties and respects reduced motion preferences.
Guides AI tools away from common visual issues such as unnecessary gradients, glow effects, and unstable layout choices.
limitations: It provides review guidance only and does not run automated accessibility, visual regression, or performance tests.
It assumes a frontend stack that can use Tailwind CSS and modern React component primitives.
It may conflict with existing brand systems that require gradients, custom spacing, or custom animation rules.
It does not include project-specific component code or a runnable validation script.

use_cases.title: Review a Generated Interface
use_cases.description: Check an AI-generated UI file for layout, animation, accessibility, and visual quality issues before merging.

use_cases.title: Set Design Guardrails
use_cases.description: Apply consistent interface rules during product UI work so generated screens match a practical quality baseline.

use_cases.title: Guide Agentic UI Builds
use_cases.description: Give an AI coding agent clear constraints before it creates or edits React and Tailwind interface code.

prompt_templates.title: Apply the Baseline
prompt_templates.prompt:
/baseline-ui
Apply the baseline UI constraints to the next interface task in this conversation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup before asking an AI tool to build a UI.

prompt_templates.title: Review One File
prompt_templates.prompt:
/baseline-ui <file>
Review this UI file and list each violation, why it matters, and the exact fix.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of a single component or page.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Review Findings
prompt_templates.prompt:
/baseline-ui <file>
Review the file, then update the implementation to fix the highest-impact accessibility, layout, and animation issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for review plus implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a UI Flow
prompt_templates.prompt:
/baseline-ui
Audit the full UI flow for component primitives, keyboard behavior, safe areas, loading states, error placement, animation limits, typography, z-index, and design restraint.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across a multi-screen feature.

output_examples.input: Review a modal component that closes on destructive delete without confirmation.
output_examples.output: The review identifies the missing AlertDialog for an irreversible action, explains the user risk, and recommends a confirmation flow using existing primitives.

output_examples.input: Review a dashboard card with animated width changes and custom gradients.
output_examples.output: The review flags layout animation and unnecessary gradients, then recommends opacity or transform animation and existing theme tokens.

output_examples.input: Apply the baseline before generating a Tailwind React settings page.
output_examples.output: The AI keeps controls accessible, uses existing primitives, adds local error states, avoids blocked paste behavior, and keeps animation minimal.

best_practices: Run the skill before generating UI so the agent follows the constraints from the start.
Use it with existing project design tokens and component primitives for best consistency.
Pair the review with real accessibility and visual checks before release.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the checklist as a substitute for user testing or accessibility testing.
Do not apply the rules blindly when a product design system gives a stronger project-specific rule.
Do not use it to justify broad rewrites unrelated to the requested UI change.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It gives AI tools a strict UI quality checklist for frontend implementation and review.

faq.question: Does it modify files by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions that an AI coding tool can apply during review or implementation.

faq.question: Which stacks does it fit best?
faq.answer: It fits React and Tailwind CSS projects that use accessible component primitives.

faq.question: Can it replace accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. It improves review discipline, but teams should still run accessibility checks and manual keyboard testing.

faq.question: Why does it mention Base UI, Radix, and React Aria?
faq.answer: Those libraries provide accessible primitives for keyboard and focus behavior.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. It is documentation-only, has no executable code, and only includes one external documentation link.
